title: Nuclear Incident
description: GET INSIDE. STAY INSIDE. STAY TUNED. Three actions that dramatically improve survival.

items.text: Know highest-risk targets in your region (military bases, major ports, large cities)
Know your best available shelter — center of large concrete building > basement > vehicle
NOAA weather radio to receive emergency broadcasts after event when internet is down
Potassium Iodide (KI) tablets stored and not expired — follow official dosing guidance
Plastic sheeting and duct tape to seal windows and doors in shelter-in-place
14-day food supply in sealed containers (fallout cannot penetrate sealed food)
14-day water supply in sealed containers stored BEFORE any event
N95 masks for each person — reduce fallout particle inhalation if caught outside
Full-coverage clothing (long sleeves, gloves, hat) for any required outdoor exposure
Battery radio to monitor official guidance — minimum 24–48 hour shelter-in-place
Understand the 7:10 rule: radiation drops 90% every 7-fold time increase after detonation
Decontamination plan: remove and bag outer clothing before entering shelter, shower if able
Do NOT look at or approach the fireball or mushroom cloud — thermal flash blinds instantly
Do not use outdoor water, open containers, or unverified municipal supply until declared safe
Out-of-area emergency contact and meeting point documented on paper — cell infrastructure may fail
